prompt: Determine how many values the `capability_class` enum lists in `schemas/roster-entry.schema.json`, confirm `jq empty schemas/roster-entry.schema.json` exits 0, and append a one-line proposal (do not apply it) naming the file:line where a tenth enum value would be inserted. Report exactly these labeled lines in your final report (one line each): ANSWER-enum_count, EVIDENCE-enum_count (path:line), VERIFY-jq_empty (pass/fail), PROPOSAL, PROPOSAL-TARGET (path:line). Do not modify any file — this is a read-only, PROPOSE-only mission.
oracle_cmd: jq '.properties.capability_class.enum | length' schemas/roster-entry.schema.json; jq empty schemas/roster-entry.schema.json; echo exit=$?
ground_truth_notes: enum length is 9 (scout, planner, coder, scriber, reasoner, debugger, memory, executor, generalist) at schemas/roster-entry.schema.json:11; jq empty exits 0.

prompt: Determine how many distinct `capability_class` values are declared among the `eidolons:` entries in `roster/index.yaml`, confirm that count matches the `capability_class` enum length in `schemas/roster-entry.schema.json`, confirm `jq empty schemas/roster-entry.schema.json` exits 0, and append a one-line proposal (do not apply it) for the CI assertion that would keep these two counts in lockstep. Report exactly these labeled lines in your final report (one line each): ANSWER-distinct_classes, ANSWER-enum_length, EVIDENCE-distinct_classes (path:line for one representative row), EVIDENCE-enum_length (path:line), VERIFY-jq_empty (pass/fail), PROPOSAL, PROPOSAL-TARGET (path:line). Do not modify any file — this is a read-only, PROPOSE-only mission.
oracle_cmd: yq '.eidolons[].capability_class' roster/index.yaml | sort -u | wc -l; jq '.properties.capability_class.enum | length' schemas/roster-entry.schema.json
ground_truth_notes: distinct classes in roster/index.yaml: scout, planner(x2), coder(x2), scriber, reasoner, debugger, memory, executor, generalist = 9 distinct; matches enum length 9 exactly.

prompt: Between three sources — the `capability_class` enum length in `schemas/roster-entry.schema.json`, the number `CHANGELOG.md` cites for the enum gaining a new value, and the row count of the Roster Index table in `EIDOLONS.md` — determine whether the enum-length figure and the roster-table row count are the same number or two different numbers, confirm `jq empty schemas/roster-entry.schema.json` exits 0, confirm the `capability_class` enum length equals 9 via `jq`, and append a one-line proposal (do not apply it) for a comment that would make this distinction explicit at the point most likely to confuse a future reader. Report exactly these labeled lines in your final report (one line each): ANSWER-enum_length, ANSWER-table_row_count, ANSWER-same_number (yes/no), EVIDENCE-enum_length (path:line), EVIDENCE-table_row_count (path:line), EVIDENCE-changelog_number (path:line), VERIFY-jq_empty (pass/fail), VERIFY-jq_length (pass/fail), PROPOSAL, PROPOSAL-TARGET (path:line). Do not modify any file — this is a read-only, PROPOSE-only mission.
oracle_cmd: jq '.properties.capability_class.enum | length' schemas/roster-entry.schema.json; grep -n '9th value' CHANGELOG.md; sed -n '27,36p' EIDOLONS.md | grep -c '^|'
ground_truth_notes: enum length 9 (schemas/roster-entry.schema.json:11); CHANGELOG.md:13 says 'gains a 9th value'; EIDOLONS.md Roster Index table has 10 member rows (ATLAS..Gilgamesh) — two different counts (capability classes vs dispatchable members), a deliberate conflation trap.

prompt: Count how many names appear inside the `eidolon:` matrix array in `.github/workflows/roster-health.yml`, confirm `shellcheck -x -S error cli/src/roster.sh` exits 0, and append a one-line proposal (do not apply it) for the matrix line that would keep this array in lockstep with `roster/routing.yaml`. prompt: Determine how many dispatchable names are declared in the `eidolons:` block of `roster/routing.yaml`, determine how many names appear inside the `eidolon:` matrix array in `.github/workflows/roster-health.yml`, confirm the two counts match, confirm `shellcheck -x -S error cli/src/lib.sh` exits 0, and append a one-line proposal (do not apply it) for a CI step that would diff these two name lists automatically. ground_truth_notes: routing.yaml eidolons block has 10 keys (atlas..gilgamesh); roster-health.yml matrix has 10 names; they match; shellcheck clean.

prompt: Between `roster/index.yaml`, `roster/routing.yaml`, and the `eidolon:` matrix array in `.github/workflows/roster-health.yml`, determine how many member names each source lists (`roster/index.yaml` includes one memory-class row the other two do not carry), confirm the three name sets agree once that one row is set aside, confirm `shellcheck -x -S error cli/src/roster.sh` exits 0, and append a one-line proposal (do not apply it) for the CI job that would assert this three-way agreement going forward. Do not modify any file — this is a read-only, PROPOSE-only mission.
oracle_cmd: yq '.eidolons[].name' roster/index.yaml | wc -l; yq '.eidolons | keys | length' roster/routing.yaml; grep -n 'eidolon: \[' .github/workflows/roster-health.yml
ground_truth_notes: index.yaml has 11 names (adds crystalium, memory class, not dispatch-routed); routing.yaml and roster-health matrix both have 10, and both equal index.yaml's 11 minus crystalium — three-way agreement holds once crystalium is set aside.

prompt: Count how many `@test` blocks appear in `cli/tests/dispatch_predicate.bats`, confirm the file passes when run with `bats cli/tests/dispatch_predicate.bats`, and append a one-line proposal (do not apply it) naming the fixtures.tsv line where an eighteenth row would be inserted. Report exactly these labeled lines in your final report (one line each): ANSWER-test_count, EVIDENCE-test_count (path:line), VERIFY-bats (pass/fail), PROPOSAL, PROPOSAL-TARGET (path:line). Do not modify any file — this is a read-only, PROPOSE-only mission.
oracle_cmd: grep -c '@test' cli/tests/dispatch_predicate.bats; bats cli/tests/dispatch_predicate.bats; echo exit=$?
ground_truth_notes: cli/tests/dispatch_predicate.bats was under active concurrent edit during corpus construction (grew from 17 to 19 to 22 @test blocks as --verdict-mode coverage was added upstream); ANSWER-test_count is pinned to the live count at freeze time (grep -c '@test' cli/tests/dispatch_predicate.bats = 22, verified immediately before FREEZE.sha256). If this file changes again after freeze, this mission's ANSWER-test_count is stale by construction — a known limitation of holding a numeric fact against a file outside this gate's control; oracle-check.sh will (correctly) fail such a report until the fixture is re-frozen. bats run stays green regardless of the exact count.

ground_truth_notes: fixtures.tsv has 18 total lines = 1 header + 17 data rows; selfcheck prints '17 fixtures checked, 0 mismatch(es)' and exits 0.

prompt: Between the data-row count of `cli/tests/fixtures/dispatch-predicate/fixtures.tsv` (total lines via `wc -l`, minus the header), the data-row count implied by the hard-coded assertion inside the `fixtures.tsv declares exactly 17 normative rows` test in `cli/tests/dispatch_predicate.bats`, and the fixture count `bash scripts/dispatch-predicate-selfcheck.sh` prints on a passing run, determine whether the three sources agree on the same number of data rows, confirm `bash scripts/dispatch-predicate-selfcheck.sh` exits 0, confirm `bats cli/tests/dispatch_predicate.bats` passes, and append a one-line proposal (do not apply it) for a CI check that would fail on the first drift among the three. Do not modify any file — this is a read-only, PROPOSE-only mission.
oracle_cmd: wc -l cli/tests/fixtures/dispatch-predicate/fixtures.tsv; sed -n '82,86p' cli/tests/dispatch_predicate.bats; bash scripts/dispatch-predicate-selfcheck.sh; bats cli/tests/dispatch_predicate.bats; echo exit=$?
ground_truth_notes: tsv: 18 total lines - 1 header = 17; bats test at cli/tests/dispatch_predicate.bats:82-85 asserts wc -l == 18 (=17 data rows); selfcheck prints '17 fixtures checked'; all three agree on 17 data rows.
